rule_text: Do not schedule back-to-back lectures for the same course. Alternate with practice, quizzes, or other activities to maintain engagement.
Do not schedule back-to-back module quizzes. Put at least one learning activity (lecture, practice, revision) between two quizzes.
If a lecture's actual content is under 30 minutes it must not occupy a 50-minute lecture slot. Flag such schedules as invalid.
The gap between a lecture and its practice session must not exceed 30 hours, so concepts are reinforced while fresh.
Pre-configured Academic Assessment slots are fixed. They cannot be modified, rescheduled, or overridden.
Pre-configured Skill Assessment slots are reserved and fixed. They cannot be modified, rescheduled, or overridden.

rule: Ensure Sufficient Revision Before Major Exams
rule_text: Do not schedule new lectures/practice/high-load activities immediately before Mid or End exams. Allocate dedicated revision time before every major exam.
Distribute the curriculum evenly across the semester. Avoid slow early weeks followed by excessive load at the end. (This is the failure mode behind most overruns.)
Do not postpone or compress foundational topics for any reason. Prerequisite technologies must be completed before dependent subjects begin.

rule: Ensure Timely Availability of Module Quizzes
rule_text: Module quizzes must become available immediately after the corresponding content is complete (or within the defined rollout window). Delayed availability is a defect.
All prerequisite lectures and practice must be complete before scheduling the corresponding Module Quiz, Skill Assessment, or Academic Assessment.
